senses.text: 1630s, "ill-made," earlier "crude, upolished" (1530s), from Latin inconditus "disordered, uncouth," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + conditus, past participle of condere "put together" (see abscond). Applied from 1845 to natural utterances ("oh!") from Latin (vox) incondita.
